statusLine: European fuel security remains under strain with 20 of 27 countries below mandatory reserves in at least one fuel type, while oil prices hold steady at $93 per barrel.
fullAnalysis:
European fuel security continues to face significant pressure, with 20 of 27 EU countries falling below the mandatory 90-day minimum reserves in at least one fuel category. The situation is most severe for petrol and jet fuel, where 17 and 19 countries respectively fail to meet minimum requirements. Diesel stocks show marginally better performance with 14 countries below thresholds, though the EU average of 88.8 days remains concerning.

Eastern European nations face the most acute challenges. Luxembourg, Poland, Austria, Romania, and Portugal all maintain critical status across multiple fuel types, with some reserves dropping to dangerously low levels - Romania's diesel stocks at just 30.2 days and Poland's jet fuel at 22.5 days represent particularly severe shortfalls. Even larger economies show strain, with France holding only 69.3 days of petrol reserves and Germany at 59.9 days for jet fuel.

The price differential across the EU remains substantial, with Netherlands consumers paying €2.347 per liter for petrol compared to Bulgaria's €1.331 - a 76% difference that reflects varying tax policies and supply chain pressures. Brent crude's stability at $93.09 per barrel provides some near-term price predictability, though this represents a significant 28.4% increase from pre-escalation levels in late February.

While some countries maintain robust reserves - Estonia and Slovenia showing exceptional jet fuel stocks at 320 and 365 days respectively - the widespread nature of shortfalls across multiple fuel types indicates systemic supply chain stress that requires coordinated European response measures.
keyPoints: 20 of 27 EU countries fall below mandatory 90-day reserves in at least one fuel type, with jet fuel showing the most widespread shortages affecting 19 countries
Eastern European nations face critical shortfalls, with Romania holding only 30.2 days of diesel reserves and Poland at 22.5 days for jet fuel
Major economies including France (69.3 days petrol) and Germany (59.9 days jet fuel) also struggle to meet minimum requirements
Fuel price disparities persist across the EU, with Netherlands petrol at €2.347/L compared to Bulgaria's €1.331/L
Oil prices remain elevated at $93.09/barrel, representing a 28.4% increase since the February escalation despite recent stability
